mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 65% of overlapping monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 2% annually only during post-recession recovery phases, where supply-side capacity utilization drives margin expansion and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2% yields negative 20-day forward returns in 70% of cases during recovery regimes, or the signal loses predictive power outside recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 1% reflects a sudden demand shock that forces immediate equity repricing and negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where RSAFS drops exceed 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with stable employment levels indicate a soft landing policy stance that preserves earnings while compressing valuation multiples, resulting in positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and stable PAYEMS produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of observations during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 per month only generates positive monthly equity returns during late-cycle expansions where wage growth supports consumer spending without triggering inflation fears.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200k yields negative 20-day returns in 65% of late-cycle expansion months, or the signal reverses direction in other regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production index declines directly reduce corporate output expectations, causing immediate negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year sample or 5-day forward returns are positive in 55% of months with declining INDPRO.
